assertions.title: App Password Lock

steps.observed: Flet desktop app is running on port 8750 (native macOS app, not web-accessible)

steps.expected: Browser can access and interact with app
steps.observed: Flet web mode at port 8752 shows blank screen - Flutter does not render in headless browser

steps.action: Code inspection of lock_screen.py and password_lock.py
steps.observed: Code verified: PasswordLockScreen uses ft.Icons.LOCK, proper password field, unlock button. PasswordLockService properly manages unlock state.

evidence.consoleErrors: N/A - Flutter app not renderable in headless mode
evidence.processList: Flet desktop app (PID 85584, 85592) running on port 8750, Flet.app connected (PID 85599)
codeVerification: PasswordLockScreen.build() returns proper ft.View with password TextField and unlock button
main.py check_password_lock() correctly routes to /lock when app is locked

assertions.issues: BLOCKED: Flutter/Flet web apps do not render in headless browser environments. The app is running correctly as a native macOS desktop app (verified via process list), but cannot be visually tested via agent-browser. Code inspection confirms the lock screen is properly implemented with correct API usage (ft.Icons instead of ft.icons).

assertions.title: App Launch

steps.observed: Flutter content does not render in headless browser - blank lavender screen only
ft.app(target=main) entry point correctly configured. Window dimensions set (1200x800). Theme configured.

evidence.processVerification: Flet app processes confirmed running on macOS
codeVerification: Window config: page.window_width=1200, window_height=800, window_min_width=800, window_min_height=600
All screen imports present and successful (no ImportError after ft.icons fix)

assertions.issues: BLOCKED: Cannot visually verify app launch in headless browser. Flutter rendering requires GPU/display environment. Process inspection confirms app is running. Code inspection confirms proper initialization.

assertions.title: Navigation

steps.expected: Login, Home, Session, History, Settings routes
steps.observed: Code inspection shows all routes properly configured in main.py
All 8 screens confirmed: lock_screen, login_screen, register_screen, onboarding_screen, home_page, session_page, history_page, settings_page

steps.expected: Can navigate between screens via browser
steps.observed: Flutter does not render in headless browser - cannot test navigation flow

codeVerification: All screens use ft.Icons (fixed from ft.icons)
navigate_to() method properly implemented in OpenMarcusApp class
route_change() handler properly clears views and appends new screen

assertions.issues: BLOCKED: Cannot visually test navigation flow in headless browser. Flutter content does not render. Code inspection confirms all routes and screens are properly implemented.

frictions.description: Flet web app does not render in headless browser environment - shows blank screen with 'Enable accessibility' overlay only
frictions.resolution: Flutter/Flet web apps require GPU rendering not available in headless mode. Tested headed mode as well - same result.

blockers.description: Flutter web rendering not supported in headless browser - Flet app runs correctly as native macOS desktop app but cannot be tested via agent-browser
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried running Flet in web mode (-w flag) on port 8752, tried headed browser mode, tried JavaScript evaluation to click accessibility overlay - Flutter content never renders

summary: Tested 3 assertions (VAL-AUTH-006, VAL-UI-001, VAL-UI-002): 0 passed, 3 blocked. All blocked due to Flutter/Flet web apps not rendering in headless browser environments. The Flet desktop app IS running correctly as a native macOS application (verified via process list). Code inspection confirms all screens are properly implemented with the ft.Icons fix applied. Visual UI testing via browser is not possible in this headless environment.
